Aiaba

System / Location · First seen 3311-07-17 · 3 mentions
EDSM ↗ Inara ↗
Active: 3311-07-17 → 3311-07-24
System Overview

Aiaba represents the kind of mid-sized independent system that rarely makes galactic headlines yet sustains the industrial base of the frontier economy. With four million inhabitants governed by the Community of the Vault under a cooperative charter, the system combined industrial manufacturing with military production — a versatile economy that attracted interest from major shipbuilders like Zorgon Peterson and Brewer Corporation.

The system's mention in July 3311 came without dramatic context, suggesting routine commercial or logistical activity rather than crisis or conflict. Located in a relatively stable sector with low security requirements, Aiaba offered the kind of predictable operational environment that manufacturers valued for testing new designs or establishing supply partnerships. Its brief appearance in the records reminds us that much of the galaxy's economic activity proceeds quietly, unmarked by the spectacular events that dominate GalNet coverage.

System Data
Coordinates: [97.3125, 51.625, 103.125]
Allegiance: Independent
Government: Cooperative
Controlling Faction: Community of the Vault
Security: Low
Economy: Industrial
Population: 4.0M

Mentions Timeline (3 articles)
3311-07-24
Panther Clipper Enters Service, Trailblazer Resupply Calls
Zorgon Peterson confirmed the Panther Clipper entered full-scale production after community support in Aiaba, while Brewer Corporation simultaneously called for pilots to resupply Trailblazer megaships at Starlace Station in Minerva.
3311-07-22
Zorgon Peterson Launches the Panther Clipper
Zorgon Peterson officially launched the Panther Clipper, the largest civilian heavy freighter with over 1,200 tonnes capacity and the first ship compatible with the MkII cargo rack, while pirate activity in Aiaba threatened production.
3311-07-17
Zorgon Peterson Seeks Support for Panther Clipper Mk II Launch
Zorgon Peterson called for pilot support to complete the Panther Clipper Mk II after pirate operations — suspected to be retributive Kumo Crew attacks following their Beta-3 Tucani defeat — disrupted production supply chains in Aiaba.
